LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MR. CROSETTI'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE STAYED AT DEFENDANT'S HOTEL. ASSUMING THAT CALIFORNIA LAW APPLIES TO THE ISSUES OF NEGLIGENCE AND NEITHER SIDE HAS ARGUED OTHERWISE, DEFENDANT'S DUTY IS TO USE REASONABLE CARE TO KEEP ITS PROPERTY IN A REASONABLY SAFE CONDITION. (CACI 1001). WHETHER DEFENDANT BREACHED THAT DUTY BY NOT PROVIDING A BATH MAT IS A QUESTION OF FACT TO BE DECIDED BY THE TRIER OF FACT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS 4-6 ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Matthew S. Conant and Maria Lampasona of Lombardi Loper & Conant, LLP (510.433.2600) for defendant and moving party; John E. Hill of the Law Offices of John E. Hill, PC (510.588.1000) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Mr. Crosetti's declaration creates a triable issue whether he stayed at defendant's hotel. Assuming that California law applies to the issues of negligence and neither side has argued otherwise, defendant's duty is to use reasonable care to keep its property in a reasonably safe condition. (CACI 1001). Whether defendant breached that duty by not providing a bath mat is a question of fact to be decided by the trier of fact. Defendant's objections 4-6 are overruled. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
